source_jobs.title: Telemedicine Psychiatrist (Mental Health Provider)
source_jobs.description:
TELEMEDICINE PSYCHIATRIST (MENTAL HEALTH PROVIDER)




OVERVIEW

We are seeking a Board-Certified Telemedicine Psychiatrist to deliver high-quality, integrated psychiatry and behavioral health services within a value-based care model. This role is ideal for physicians motivated by improving outcomes, reducing total cost of care, and delivering proactive, longitudinal patient management through a virtual-first platform.

Physicians will provide comprehensive care across both physical and behavioral health, managing acute and chronic conditions while addressing underlying drivers of utilization, including mental health and social factors. Care is delivered through scheduled and on-demand encounters, with all interactions documented in the Doctronic platform.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Deliver whole-person, longitudinal care that integrates primary care and behavioral health, with a focus on measurable outcomes.

 - Manage acute and chronic conditions, emphasizing the reduction of avoidable ED visits, hospitalizations, and specialty leakage.

 - Conduct behavioral health screening, diagnosis, and front-line management (e.g., anxiety, depression, ADHD, substance use), integrating care into overall treatment plans.

 - Prescribe and manage both medical and psychotropic medications in accordance with evidence-based guidelines.

 - Utilize data-driven, measurement-based care (e.g., PHQ-9, GAD-7, utilization patterns) to guide clinical decisions and track patient progress.

 - Identify and address high-risk patients and rising-risk populations through proactive outreach and care interventions.

 - Perform risk stratification and care planning to improve quality metrics and patient outcomes.

 - Collaborate with a multidisciplinary care team (behavioral health specialists, care coordinators, and support staff) to ensure coordinated, high-value care delivery.

 - Document all patient interactions and care plans in the Doctronic EHR, ensuring accuracy for quality reporting and value-based reimbursement.

 - Maintain compliance with HIPAA, telehealth regulations, and state-specific prescribing requirements.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Active, unrestricted medical license in at least 40 U.S. states (50 states preferred).

 - Board Certification in Family Medicine or Internal Medicine required.

 - Demonstrated experience in integrated care delivery, including management of common behavioral health conditions within a primary care setting.

 - Strong understanding of value-based care principles, including quality metrics, utilization management, and total cost of care.

 - Experience managing chronic conditions and high-risk patient populations in a longitudinal care model.

 - Comfortable prescribing and managing psychotropic medications within a primary care scope.

 - Experience with telemedicine platforms and delivering high-quality virtual care.

 - Excellent communication skills with a patient-centered, outcomes-driven approach.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in value-based care environments (e.g., Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, ACOs, employer-sponsored populations).

 - Familiarity with quality measures such as HEDIS, STAR ratings, and other performance-based metrics.

 - Training or experience in behavioral health, addiction medicine, or collaborative care models (CoCM).

 - Experience with data-driven care, population health tools, and outcomes tracking.

 - Multi-state licensure via IMLCC strongly preferred.

 - Prior experience in a virtual-first or digital health organization.




SCHEDULE & COMMITMENT

 - Minimum weekly scheduling of 40 hours required.

 - Must be willing to work evenings, weekends, and occasional overnight shifts to support 24/7 patient access.




DESIRED STATE LICENSES

 - Minimum: IMLCC states plus California and New York

 - Preferred: All 50 states plus Washington, D.C.

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, Enterprise
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT LEAD, ENTERPRISE




WHERE MEDICINE MEETS INTELLIGENCE

Doctronic is the first AI legally authorized to practice medicine. We're processing millions of consultations monthly with 99%+ treatment plan accuracy validated by board-certified clinicians.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Product Lead, Enterprise to own our enterprise product strategy and platform offerings. You'll be responsible for enterprise integrations, our API product, embedded AI configuration, and customer-specific roadmap prioritization that serves health systems, payers, and strategic partners.

This role requires exceptional product judgment: knowing when to build custom solutions and when to hold the line for scalability. You'll translate diverse enterprise customer needs into product decisions that work for many, not just one. Your work will enable Doctronic's AI to reach millions of patients through our partners' systems.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own the enterprise integration strategy and API product roadmap

 - Design and ship integrations for health systems, payers, and strategic healthcare partners

 - Build embedded AI configuration tools that allow partners to customize Doctronic for their needs

 - Manage customer-specific roadmap prioritization across a complex enterprise portfolio

 - Translate bespoke customer requests into scalable product features that serve the broader platform

 - Partner with sales to scope technical feasibility during enterprise deals

 - Work closely with engineering to build robust, well-documented APIs and integration frameworks

 - Collaborate with customer success to ensure smooth implementation and ongoing partner satisfaction

 - Balance competing stakeholder needs while maintaining platform integrity and scalability

 - Define platform metrics and success criteria for enterprise partnerships


WHO YOU ARE

 - 5+ years of product management experience with a focus on enterprise products

 - Skilled at saying no to one-off customizations while finding the 80% solution that works for many

 - Strong technical fluency with APIs, integrations, and platform architecture

 - Excellent at translating enterprise customer needs into scalable product requirements

 - Experienced in managing complex stakeholder landscapes across sales, engineering, and customer success

 - Comfortable navigating enterprise sales cycles and customer dynamics

 - Strategic thinker who can balance short-term customer requests with long-term platform vision

 - Clear communicator who can explain technical trade-offs to both technical and non-technical audiences


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience building API products or developer platforms

 - Background in healthcare IT, EMR integrations, or health system partnerships

 - Familiarity with HL7, FHIR, or other healthcare data standards

 - Previous experience with enterprise SaaS or B2B marketplace products

 - Understanding of healthcare compliance requirements (HIPAA, BAA, etc.)

 - Track record of scaling platform products from early customers to broad adoption




WHAT WE OFFER

Fully Remote Team
Work where you thrive—at home, in a co-working space, or anywhere in the world.

Competitive Compensation
Your work should be valued—today and in the future. We offer competitive salaries along with equity opportunities, so you're not just contributing to our growth—you're sharing in it.

Comprehensive Health Benefits
We offer comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage—plus mental health support and flexible time off—because caring for others starts with caring for ourselves.

Building AI That Matters
Join Doctronic and work with cutting-edge AI that's transforming healthcare and helping people make faster, smarter decisions.

Doctronic is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

source_jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR BACKEND ENGINEER

Doctronic | New York City ONSITE (Hybrid) | Reports to CEO




THE MISSION

Let's make healthcare free and accessible to everyone through AI. We need backend infrastructure that can scale our AI doctor platform to billions of consultations while maintaining medical safety and HIPAA compliance.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD

Healthcare API Infrastructure

 - Scalable APIs using Python, FastAPI, AWS handling millions of monthly requests

 - RESTful API development supporting our AI healthcare tools

 - Real-time data processing for sensitive patient information (HIPAA-compliant)

 - Internal API architecture for web platform and AI coordination

Integration & Data Systems

 - Secure health record exchange with EHR systems, QHINs, healthcare networks

 - Third-party integrations: telehealth providers, analytics platforms, healthcare APIs

 - Large-scale sensitive data processing with encryption and compliance safeguards

 - Partner API integrations across dozens of diverse healthcare systems

Scale & Reliability

 - Database optimization for high-volume healthcare data with complex queries

 - Redis for secure caching and session management in healthcare environments

 - Error handling, failover systems, disaster recovery for mission-critical services

 - Performance optimization for rapid user growth

AI System Integration

 - Backend coordination for AI tools and decision-making workflows

 - Python-based deterministic systems working alongside AI agents for clinical accuracy

 - APIs supporting model inference, data preprocessing, result processing

 - Infrastructure for AI safety: validation layers and human escalation protocols

You'll also work on healthcare compliance automation, advanced monitoring, multi-region deployment, and backend foundation for next-generation AI healthcare features.




HOW WE WORK

 - Engineering-first org: no project managers, just senior engineers

 - Flat structure with direct CEO reporting and high autonomy

 - Own features end-to-end from minimal specs to production

 - AI coding tools encouraged

 - Ship fast with good judgment; daily standups for alignment, not micromanagement




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Education & Background

 - CS degree OR equivalent demonstrated expertise

 - Engineering, Math, or related technical degrees welcomed with strong programming foundation

 - Preference for graduates from top programs at top universities

 - Preference for founders, founding engineers, or extensive startup experience

 - Independent problem solver who collaborates well cross-functionally

Technical Expertise

 - 6+ years backend engineering with Python and API development

 - FastAPI, Flask, or similar Python web frameworks

 - Cloud services experience

 - RESTful APIs, high-volume traffic handling

 - Relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and optimization

 - Redis, caching strategies, distributed systems design

System Design & Integration

 - Third-party API integrations and webhook management

 - Microservices architecture and distributed system patterns

 - Security best practices for sensitive data and API design

 - CI/CD pipelines, containerization (Docker), deployment automation

 - Monitoring, logging, observability tools




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Systems for healthcare, fintech, or regulated industries

 - Healthcare data standards (HL7, FHIR) and HIPAA compliance

 - High-scale data processing and real-time systems

 - AI/ML systems and model serving infrastructure

 - Message queues, event-driven architectures, async processing

 - Healthcare workflows, EHR systems, telehealth platforms

 - Seed/Series A experience in healthcare or technology

 - Advanced degree in CS, Engineering, or related field




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary plus significant equity

 - Build infrastructure transforming healthcare accessibility for millions

 - Direct CEO reporting with significant autonomy

 - Access to leading technologies and healthcare experts

 - Be

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Healthcare is broken. We're fixing it. Millions of people already trust our AI doctor — and we're just getting started. To keep building at this pace, we need someone who can find and close the brilliant engineers who are shaping what AI can do for human health.

This is Doctronic's first technical recruiting hire. You won't be inheriting a process — you'll be building one. You'll work directly with engineering leadership and the CEO to define how we attract, evaluate, and bring in exceptional AI and engineering talent. If you've ever wanted to own a function from scratch at a company doing something that genuinely matters, this is it.

 

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own full-cycle recruiting for Senior AI/ML Engineers, Backend Engineers, and other technical roles from sourcing to offer close

 - Build and run a proactive sourcing strategy to reach passive candidates — especially those working at the frontier of AI research and applied ML

 - Partner with engineering leads to deeply understand role requirements, technical signals, and what “exceptional” looks like at Doctronic

 - Design and iterate on our recruiting process, candidate experience, and employer brand — from scratch

 - Develop pipelines for contested, hard-to-find talent: LLM engineers, clinical AI specialists, agentic systems builders

 - Track metrics, manage your pipeline in an ATS, and report on funnel health to leadership

 

MUST-HAVES

• 5+ years of technical recruiting, with at least 2 years focused on AI/ML or applied ML roles

• Proven track record closing senior AI engineers in a competitive market — people who had other offers

• Ability to hold a substantive conversation about model architectures, LLM fine-tuning, agentic systems, and inference pipelines

• Experience building recruiting infrastructure at an early-stage startup (no playbook, no coordinator, no excuses)

• Strong sourcing instincts — you know where the engineers who never apply are, and how to reach them

• Based in New York; this role is hybrid onsite (in office 3 days a week)

 

NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience recruiting for healthcare tech, clinical AI, or regulated-industry engineering teams

 - A network in NYC’s AI and ML engineering community

 - Prior experience as the first or second recruiter at a high-growth startup

 - Familiarity with Python, FastAPI, or ML frameworks — enough to read a GitHub profile intelligently

 

LIFE AT DOCTRONIC

Competitive comp Salary + equity — you grow with us

Flexible time off We trust you to manage your time

Made in NYC Hybrid Onsite in New York with a world-class team

 

WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We hire for talent, passion, and a relentless drive to do excellent work. You don’t need to have done every thing on this list — but you do need to be exceptional at the core of it: finding engineers who are hard to find, earning their trust, and closing them on a mission that’s worth their talent.

If you are exceptional — the best of the best — we want you.

source_jobs.title: Senior Frontend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR FRONTEND ENGINEER

Doctronic | New York City ONSITE (Hybrid) | Reports to CEO




THE MISSION

Let's make healthcare free and accessible to everyone through AI. We need frontend engineers to build intuitive, fast interfaces that make complex medical interactions feel simple across web, iOS, and Android.




WHAT YOU'LL BUILD

Cross-Platform Applications

 - React web application serving millions of patient consultations

 - Native iOS app (Swift) and Android app (Kotlin) for optimal performance

 - Embeddable web product for partner healthcare systems

 - Shared component libraries and design systems across platforms

Patient Experience

 - Real-time chat interfaces for AI doctor consultations

 - Medical data visualization (symptoms, medications, health records)

 - Video/voice consultation interfaces integrated with telehealth systems

 - Onboarding flows handling identity verification and insurance intake

Healthcare Data UI

 - Complex forms for medical history, medications, insurance information

 - EHR data display with FHIR-compliant structures

 - Document generation and PDF rendering for clinical notes

 - Multi-language support for global accessibility

Performance & Accessibility

 - Sub-second load times for critical patient interactions

 - Offline-first architecture for unreliable connections

 - WCAG compliance for healthcare accessibility requirements

 - HIPAA-compliant data handling in frontend applications

You'll also work on AI response streaming, rich text medical documentation, prescription management interfaces, and the foundation for voice/video AI avatars.




HOW WE WORK

 - Engineering-first org: no project managers, just senior engineers

 - Flat structure with direct CEO reporting and high autonomy

 - Own features end-to-end from minimal specs to production

 - AI coding tools encouraged

 - Ship fast with good judgment; daily standups for alignment, not micromanagement




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Education & Background

 - CS degree OR equivalent demonstrated expertise

 - Related technical degrees welcomed with strong programming foundation

 - Preference for graduates from top programs at top universities

 - Preference for founders, founding engineers, or extensive startup experience

 - Independent problem solver who collaborates well cross-functionally

Technical Expertise

 - 5+ years frontend engineering with expertise in React

 - Production React web applications at scale

 - Native mobile development (Swift for iOS and/or Kotlin for Android)

 - Modern JavaScript/TypeScript, state management (Redux, Context)

 - RESTful API integration and real-time data handling

 - Responsive design and cross-browser compatibility

Design & UX

 - Strong UI/UX sensibility for healthcare applications

 - Component-based architecture and reusable design systems

 - Performance optimization for web and mobile

 - Accessibility standards (WCAG) and inclusive design principles




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Applications for healthcare, fintech, or regulated industries

 - HIPAA compliance requirements for frontend applications

 - Real-time chat or messaging interfaces at scale

 - Video/voice communication integration (WebRTC, native APIs)

 - Embeddable widget or SDK development

 - Healthcare workflows, patient portals, or medical applications

 - Seed/Series A experience in healthcare or technology

 - Advanced degree in CS, HCI, or related field




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary plus significant equity

 - Build interfaces transforming healthcare accessibility for millions

 - Direct CEO reporting with significant autonomy

 - Access to leading technologies and healthcare experts

 - Benefits reimbursement

Funding & Momentum

 - Union Square Ventures led our seed (Feb 2025)

 - Lightspeed Venture Partners preempted our Series A six months later (Aug 2025)

 - Abstract preempted our Series B five months later (Jan 2026)

 - Significant equity stake in fast-moving, well-backed company

source_jobs.title: Clinical Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
CLINICAL PRODUCT MANAGER

Doctronic Physicians Group (DPG) & Doctronic Inc.




POSITION OVERVIEW

We seek a physician-product leader to drive the development of clinical pathways and workflows that power AI-native care delivery. This role sits at the intersection of clinical medicine, product management, and partner success—requiring deep medical expertise, proven product leadership, and the ability to translate complex clinical needs into scalable AI-enabled solutions for both our internal clinic and growing network of clinical partners.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


CLINICAL PRODUCT DEVELOPMENT (50%)

 - Own the product roadmap for clinical pathways, defining and prioritizing features that enable safe, effective AI-native care delivery

 - Design and iterate on clinical workflows for urgent care, primary care, chronic disease management, and medication management use cases

 - Translate clinical requirements into detailed product specifications for our AI engineering and context engineering teams

 - Drive product decisions grounded in clinical evidence, regulatory requirements, and real-world physician workflows


PARTNER INTEGRATION & CUSTOMIZATION (35%)

 - Serve as the primary liaison for new clinical partners, leading discovery to understand their unique workflow requirements and use cases

 - Develop customized pathway configurations that adapt our AI platform to partner-specific clinical environments

 - Build scalable frameworks for partner onboarding that balance customization with product consistency

 - Gather and synthesize partner feedback to inform product roadmap priorities

 - Ensure successful deployment and adoption of clinical workflows across diverse partner settings


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL LEADERSHIP (15%)

 - Collaborate daily with AI engineering, context engineering, and clinical operations to bring clinical products from concept to deployment

 - Partner with clinical leadership to ensure pathways meet quality standards and align with evidence-based medicine

 - Work with engineering on EHR integrations and interoperability requirements for partner implementations

 - Define success metrics and evaluation frameworks for clinical pathway performance

 - Coordinate with compliance and legal teams to ensure all products meet regulatory requirements




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS


MEDICAL BACKGROUND

 - MD, DO, or equivalent medical degree (minimum requirement)

 - Completed residency strongly preferred; post-residency clinical practice experience ideal

 - Deep understanding of clinical decision-making, care delivery workflows, and physician needs

 - Experience in urgent care, primary care, emergency medicine, or internal medicine preferred


PRODUCT MANAGEMENT EXPERIENCE

 - 3+ years of product management experience, ideally in healthcare technology or digital health

 - Demonstrated ability to own product roadmaps from strategy through execution

 - Experience writing product requirements, user stories, and detailed specifications

 - Track record of shipping products that required cross-functional coordination


TECHNICAL APTITUDE

 - Strong understanding of AI/ML systems and their application in healthcare settings

 - Experience deploying or implementing AI-enabled products in production environments preferred

 - Familiarity with EHR systems, clinical data standards (HL7, FHIR), and healthcare interoperability

 - Comfort with technical discussions and ability to collaborate effectively with engineering teams


LEADERSHIP & COMMUNICATION

 - Exceptional stakeholder management skills with ability to navigate complex partner relationships

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills for both clinical and technical audiences

 - Experience working with or managing multidisciplinary teams

 - Ability to thrive in ambiguity and drive clarity in fast-moving environments




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Previous experience at healthcare technology companies, digital health startups, or health systems innova

source_jobs.title: Medical Director, Doctronic
source_jobs.description:
MEDICAL DIRECTOR, DOCTRONIC




ROLE SUMMARY

Doctronic is building an AI-native virtual medical practice. The Medical Director will partner closely with the CMO to turn clinical strategy into production systems, integrating our clinical-grade AI, practice-wide pathways and guidelines, continuous improvement of our care delivery model, and an innovative quality and safety program.

This is a builder role for a physician leader who wants to design care models, ship operational change, work directly with product teams to launch new clinical offerings, and redefine how medical care is delivered in the era of clinical-grade AI.




REPORTING AND KEY RELATIONSHIPS

 - Reports to the Chief Medical Officer

 - Works closely with Clinical Product, Clinical Operations, Quality and Compliance, Data and Analytics, and Engineering

 - Partners with supervising and practicing clinicians across the practice




CORE RESPONSIBILITIES


PRACTICE GUIDELINES AND PATHWAYS

 - Own the lifecycle of clinical pathways and practice guidelines from drafting through rollout and iteration

 - Create concise, AI-native, usable protocols that drive consistent, high-quality virtual care

 - Define escalation criteria, exclusion criteria, referral pathways, and safety thresholds for virtual-first care

 - Ensure guidelines are communicated and implemented through training, documentation standards, and workflow integration

 - Ensure guidelines are deployed through an AI-friendly interface


IMPROVE DOCTRONIC’S AI-NATIVE CARE DELIVERY MODEL

 - Continuously refine how care is delivered across intake, triage, assessment, treatment planning, follow-up, and escalation

 - Identify operational bottlenecks and redesign workflows to improve quality, speed, and patient experience

 - Standardize high-performing clinical practices and reduce unwarranted variation

 - Assist with QA and performance review of Doctronic Physician Group providers


BUILD NEW CARE OFFERINGS WITH CLINICAL PRODUCT

 - Assist the product team with clinical design for new programs and condition pathways
   
   - Define eligibility, care cadence, clinical requirements, safety guardrails, and success metrics
   
   - Partner with Product and Engineering to translate clinical logic into usable product and workflow experiences
   
   - Drive clinical readiness for launches and ensure safe, coherent execution at go-live


QUALITY AND SAFETY WORKSTREAMS

 - Lead practice quality and patient safety initiatives across the care model

 - Maintain a functioning safety event pipeline and review process with timely corrective actions

 - Run continuous learning loops through structured case review and quality audits

 - Define quality metrics, monitoring dashboards, and improvement targets with Analytics


CARE DELIVERY

 - Maintain a practice within the Doctronic AI-Native Care platform


ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES


ASSIST WITH THE CLINICAL DESIGN OF NEW AI PATHWAYS

 - Assist the product team with clinical design for new programs and condition pathways
   
   - Define eligibility, care cadence, clinical requirements, safety guardrails, and success metrics
   
   - Partner with product and engineering to translate clinical logic into usable product and workflow experiences
   
   - Drive clinical readiness for launches and ensure safe, coherent execution at go-live




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - MD or DO with active or eligible U.S. licensure

 - Board certification in Family Medicine, Internal Medicine, or Emergency Medicine strongly preferred

 - Minimum 5 years of post-residency care delivery

 - Experience supervising virtual care at scale

 - Demonstrated ability to design clinical protocols and implement them operationally

 - Experience leading quality improvement, patient safety, or clinical operations work

 - The ability or willingness to maximize productivity using AI tools


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience partnering with product and engineering teams to ship clinical workflows a

source_jobs.title: DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
DEVOPS ENGINEER


Where Medicine Meets Intelligence

Doctronic is the first AI legally authorized to practice medicine. We're processing millions of consultations monthly with 99%+ treatment plan accuracy validated by board-certified clinicians.

About the Role

We're looking for a DevOps Engineer to own our infrastructure. We're HIPAA-compliant and SOC 2 Type II certified — you'll maintain and strengthen that foundation as we scale to serve millions of patients and enterprise partners.

This role is critical to our mission. When healthcare consultations depend on your infrastructure, reliability isn't just best practice — it's a sacred responsibility. You'll combine hands-on technical work with strategic infrastructure leadership, ensuring Doctronic remains the most trusted AI diagnostic platform in healthcare.

What You'll Build

Cloud Infrastructure

 - Design, deploy, and maintain AWS infrastructure using ECS and core AWS services, including EC2, IAM, VPC, ALB/NLB, CloudWatch, ECR, S3, RDS, Glue.

 - Operate and scale production Kubernetes clusters with Helm-based application deployments.

 - Implement GitOps workflows with Argo CD to enable secure, automated, and auditable releases.

Infrastructure as Code & CI/CD

 - Provision and manage cloud infrastructure using Terraform.

 - Build and optimize CI/CD pipelines with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or similar platforms.

 - Automate infrastructure and operational workflows to improve reliability and reduce manual effort.

AI/ML & LLM Infrastructure

 - Deploy, maintain, and optimize infrastructure for AI/ML services and Large Language Models (LLMs).

 - Support scalable inference workloads with a focus on performance, reliability, and cost efficiency.

 - Collaborate with engineering teams to deliver production-ready AI platforms.

Networking & Security

 - Design and manage cloud networking, including VPCs, VPNs, Load Balancers, DNS, routing, and secure connectivity.

 - Integrate SIEM solutions to improve infrastructure visibility and incident response.

 - Implement security best practices, identity management, and least-privilege access across cloud environments.

Observability & Reliability

 - Build monitoring, logging, and alerting solutions using Prometheus, Grafana, CloudWatch.

 - Improve platform reliability through proactive monitoring, incident response, and performance optimization.

 - Build and optimize containerized workloads using Docker.

 - Administer Linux-based production environments.

 - Automate operational tasks and infrastructure management using Bash and Python.



Who You Are

 - 5+ years of experience as a DevOps Engineer or Site Reliability Engineer.

 - Strong hands-on experience with AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, Docker, Helm, and Argo CD.

 - Proven experience designing and operating highly available, cloud-native production infrastructure.

 - Solid understanding of Linux administration, networking, cloud security, and Infrastructure as Code principles.

 - Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines and deployment automation.

 - Familiarity with monitoring, logging, and observability platforms.

 - Experience supporting AI/ML workloads or modern distributed systems is a strong advantage.

 - Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot complex production environments.

 - Comfortable working in cross-functional teams and collaborating closely with software engineers, security teams, and product stakeholders.

 - Passionate about automation, reliability, scalability, and operational excellence.



Nice to Have

 - Experience with GPU infrastructure and AI/ML model deployment.

 - Familiarity with DevSecOps practices, vulnerability management, and compliance frameworks.

 - Experience with multi-cluster Kubernetes environments.

 - Knowledge of performance tuning, autoscaling, and cost optimization in AWS.

 - Experience supporting high-traffic, mission-critical production systems.

 - Understanding of disaster recov

source_jobs.title: Senior AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR AI ENGINEER

Doctronic | New York City ONSITE (Hybrid) | Reports to CEO

Let's make healthcare free and accessible to everyone through AI. We're building an AI doctor that already handles millions of consultations - our goal is to scale this to billions while maintaining medical safety and accuracy.

Core AI Systems

 - RAG pipelines processing thousands of clinical guidelines built for LLM consumption

 - Real-time inference systems handling millions of medical consultations each month

 - Custom workflows for domain-specific medical reasoning

Clinical Data Infrastructure

 - Published research in AI/ML, especially healthcare or safety-critical applications

 - Seed/Series A experience in AI or healthcare

 - Advanced degree in CS, AI/ML, or related field




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary plus significant equity

 - Build AI systems transforming healthcare for millions

 - Direct CEO reporting with significant autonomy

Funding & Momentum

 - Lightspeed Venture Partners preempted our Series A six months later (Aug 2025)

 - Abstract preempted our Series B five months later (Jan 2026)

 - Significant equity stake in fast-moving, well-backed company

source_jobs.title: Information Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
INFORMATION SECURITY ENGINEER




WHERE MEDICINE MEETS INTELLIGENCE

Doctronic is the first AI legally authorized to practice medicine. We're processing millions of consultations monthly with 99%+ treatment plan accuracy validated by board-certified clinicians.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for an Information Security Engineer to own our security posture. We're HIPAA-compliant and SOC 2 Type II certified—you'll maintain and strengthen that foundation as we scale to serve millions of patients and enterprise partners.

This role is critical to our mission. When you're protecting healthcare data, security isn't just best practice—it's a sacred responsibility. You'll combine hands-on technical work with strategic security leadership, ensuring Doctronic remains the most trusted AI diagnostic platform in healthcare.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Maintain SOC 2 Type II compliance and manage ongoing audits with external assessors

 - Implement and monitor HIPAA technical safeguards across our infrastructure and applications

 - Conduct and coordinate regular penetration testing, vulnerability assessments, and security reviews

 - Complete vendor security reviews and respond to enterprise security questionnaires from health systems and payers

 - Implement and enforce security policies across engineering, operations, and business teams

 - Respond to security incidents with urgency and thoroughness, conducting post-incident analysis

 - Build security automation and monitoring to scale protection as the company grows

 - Collaborate with engineering teams to embed security best practices into the development lifecycle

 - Stay current on emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and regulatory requirements in healthcare technology


WHO YOU ARE

 - 7+ years of information security experience in production environments

 - Healthcare or fintech background required—you understand regulated industry security requirements

 - Hands-on technical ability, not just policy and paperwork—you can read code, configure systems, and investigate incidents

 - Deep experience with SOC 2, HIPAA, or equivalent compliance frameworks

 - Familiarity with AWS security controls, IAM, encryption, and cloud security best practices

 - Strong communicator who can translate security requirements for technical and non-technical audiences

 - Proactive problem-solver who anticipates risks before they materialize

 - Collaborative partner who enables teams to move fast while staying secure


NICE TO HAVE

 - CISSP, CISM, CISA, or equivalent security certification

 - Experience with health information exchanges, TEFCA, QHIN, or interoperability standards

 - Startup security experience—building security programs from scratch vs. maintaining established ones

 - Familiarity with AI/ML security considerations and model protection

 - Experience with mobile app security (iOS/Android)

 - Knowledge of medical device security standards or FDA digital health guidance

 - Background in application security, secure SDLC, or DevSecOps


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

Base Salary: $180K-$240K + Equity

New York City | On-site
Join our NYC team and work directly with engineering and product teams to build security into everything we do.

Equity Opportunities
Share in Doctronic's growth as we transform healthcare with AI.

Comprehensive Health Benefits
We offer comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage—plus mental health support and flexible time off—because caring for others starts with caring for ourselves.

Building AI That Matters
Join Doctronic and work with cutting-edge AI that's transforming healthcare and helping people make faster, smarter decisions.




REPORTS TO

Director of Engineering

source_jobs.title: FP&A Manager
source_jobs.description:
Doctronic | Financial Planning & Analysis



FP&A Manager

New York City, NY • On-site • Reports to Director of Finance



About Doctronic

Doctronic is the first AI legally authorized to practice medicine. We are processing millions of consultations monthly with 99%+ treatment plan accuracy validated by board-certified clinicians. As we scale through our next growth phase, we are building the operational infrastructure to match our ambitions.

About the Role

We are looking for a FP&A Manager to sit at the intersection of FP&A and business operations. This is a high-ownership, hands-on role supporting the Director of Finance across financial controls, vendor management, corporate administration, and compliance operations. You will build and run the systems that keep Doctronic financially disciplined and operationally tight as we grow.



What You’ll Do

Financial Planning & Controls

 - Maintain an ongoing, dynamic budget review process - not a static 12-month cycle - so leadership always has a current picture of where we stand

 - Prepare and maintain budget vs. actuals reporting, driving alignment and accountability with senior management

 - Understand and report on weekly cash position to ensure accurate, timely visibility into cash
   
   

Ramp & AP Management

 - Own the end-to-end Ramp workflow from draft invoice ingestion through approval-ready bill preparation

 - Identify and implement process improvements that reduce manual work and accelerate the AP cycle

 - Ensure invoices are clean, coded, and routed correctly before hitting the approval queue



Vendor Management

 - Develop and maintain a procurement process that defines who can authorize spend at various thresholds

 - Manage vendor renewal calendars and ensure no material contracts auto-renew without review

 - Build cost models capturing fixed vs. variable costs, contract terms, and payment structure (upfront vs. installment)

 - Coordinate with legal counsel on vendor and consultant contracts as needed



Corporate Administration

 - Support the R&D tax credit process by gathering and organizing relevant data and documentation

 - Collect and maintain financial and operational data needed for reporting or compliance such as investor reporting, tax filing, etc.

 - Complete corporate forms and filings (e.g., new banking relationships or payment processor changes)

 - Assist HR on corporate administration tasks such as address changes and entity-level updates



Insurance Management

 - Own all business insurance applications, renewals, and policy management

 - Manage insurance requirements specific to healthcare operations, including MSO/PC structure coverage

 - Coordinate with brokers and carriers to ensure adequate and cost-effective coverage at all times
   
   

Who You Are

 - 3–5 years of experience in finance, operations, or a finance-adjacent role - ideally at a startup or high-growth company

 - 2 years of investment banking experience at a top financial institution or comparably rigorous program

 - Highly organized and process-minded: you build systems, not workarounds

 - Comfortable owning a wide scope of work independently, with a bias toward action

 - Detail-oriented with high standards for accuracy, especially in financial reporting and contracts

 - Strong communicator who can work cross-functionally with legal, HR, and senior leadership

 - Proficient with financial tools and platforms; familiarity with Ramp, QuickBooks, or similar systems is a plus

 - Comfortable navigating ambiguity and building structure in a fast-moving environment
   

Compensation & Benefits

 - Competitive base salary + equity

 - Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage

 - Mental health support and flexible time off

 - On-site in New York City, working directly alongside executive leadership

 - The chance to build financial and operational infrastructure at a company transforming healthcare with AI



Reports to: Director of Finance

Location: New

source_jobs.title: QA Engineer
source_jobs.description:
QA ENGINEER




WHERE MEDICINE MEETS INTELLIGENCE

Doctronic is the first AI legally authorized to practice medicine. We're processing millions of consultations monthly with 99%+ treatment plan accuracy validated by board-certified clinicians.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a QA Engineer to ensure the quality and reliability of our AI-powered healthcare platform. You'll be responsible for testing critical user flows, validating AI diagnostic outputs, and maintaining the high standards our patients and clinicians depend on.

This role is crucial to our mission. When you're testing healthcare software, bugs aren't just inconvenient—they can impact real medical decisions. You'll build comprehensive test strategies that cover web, mobile, telehealth integrations, and AI model outputs, ensuring every release meets our quality bar.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own end-to-end testing for web and mobile applications, from onboarding flows to telehealth visits

 - Develop and maintain automated test suites that scale with our product velocity

 - Test AI diagnostic outputs for accuracy, consistency, and edge cases across medical conditions

 - Validate integrations with third-party systems including scheduling, video platforms, and enterprise APIs

 - Create and execute test plans for new features, working closely with product and engineering teams

 - Identify, document, and track bugs through resolution, advocating for quality throughout the development cycle

 - Build testing frameworks and processes that enable the team to move fast without breaking things

 - Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations and security standards (HIPAA, SOC 2)

 - Monitor production systems and user reports to catch issues before they impact patients


WHO YOU ARE

 - 3+ years of QA engineering experience, ideally with web and mobile applications

 - Strong foundation in both manual and automated testing methodologies

 - Experience with testing frameworks and tools (Selenium, Cypress, Playwright, or similar)

 - Comfortable testing APIs and backend systems, not just user interfaces

 - Detail-oriented with excellent documentation and communication skills

 - Able to think critically about edge cases and potential failure modes

 - Collaborative team player who can work effectively with engineers, designers, and product managers

 - Passionate about quality and user experience in high-stakes environments


NICE TO HAVE

 - Healthcare or regulated industry testing experience

 - Familiarity with AI/ML model testing and validation

 - Experience with video communication platforms or real-time systems

 - Background in security testing and compliance validation

 - Understanding of mobile app testing for iOS and Android

 - Experience building QA processes from the ground up in a startup environment

 - Knowledge of performance and load testing


WHAT WE OFFER

Fully Remote Team
Work where you thrive—at home, in a co-working space, or anywhere in the world.

Competitive Compensation
Your work should be valued—today and in the future. We offer competitive salaries along with equity opportunities, so you're not just contributing to our growth—you're sharing in it.

Comprehensive Health Benefits
We offer comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage—plus mental health support and flexible time off—because caring for others starts with caring for ourselves.

Building AI That Matters
Join Doctronic and work with cutting-edge AI that's transforming healthcare and helping people make faster, smarter decisions.

Doctronic is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

source_jobs.title: Senior Clinical Context Engineer
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR CLINICAL CONTEXT ENGINEER

Doctronic Physicians Group (DPG) & Doctronic Inc.




POSITION OVERVIEW

We seek a physician-leader to bridge clinical medicine and artificial intelligence. This role combines hands-on context engineering with strategic leadership, requiring both deep medical knowledge and comfort with AI/LLM technologies.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Medical AI Development (50%)

 - Write and refine medical guidelines and prompts that enable our LLMs to provide accurate clinical responses

 - Translate medical knowledge into structured formats for our agentic workflows and knowledge graph

 - Ensure AI outputs align with current medical standards across urgent care, primary care, chronic disease management, and medication management

Systems & Evaluation (30%)

 - Organize medical databases supporting our AI agents and consensus models

 - Develop evaluation frameworks to assess LLM performance in clinical contexts

 - Create metrics specific to diagnosis accuracy, treatment appropriateness, and prescription safety

Cross-functional Collaboration (20%)

 - Day-to-day interaction with engineering team to develop and refine architecture

 - Gather feedback from our physician network to identify improvement opportunities

 - Partner with engineering on integration between AI doctor and EHR system





REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

Medical Background

 - MD, DO, or equivalent; completed residency in any specialty

 - Strong clinical decision-making skills with experience in urgent care, primary care, or internal medicine preferred

Technical Expertise

 - Data science background and a deep understanding of managing large databases

 - Demonstrated deep knowledge of AI and large language models

 - Experience with or strong interest in prompt engineering

 - Comfort with iterative testing and rapid prototyping

 - Familiarity with EHR systems and clinical workflows

Leadership Skills

 - Proven ability to manage multidisciplinary teams

 - Exceptional writing and editorial skills for medical content

 - Experience developing clinical protocols or guidelines


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Previous experience at healthcare technology companies or digital health startups

 - Understanding of HIPAA compliance and healthcare data privacy


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Direct impact on healthcare accessibility for millions of patients

 - Opportunity to shape AI medical standards at a pioneering company

 - Work with world-class AI technology unique in the industry

 - Competitive equity package

 - Salary commensurate with experience




ABOUT DOCTRONIC

Doctronic is the #1 AI Doctor, revolutionizing direct-to-patient healthcare through our proprietary AI system. Doctronic is the first AI legally authorized to practice medicine. We're processing millions of consultations monthly with 99%+ treatment plan accuracy validated by board-certified clinicians. Our platform (doctronic.ai http://doctronic.ai) provides free, anonymous, HIPAA-compliant AI consultations alongside affordable human physician visits ($40) available 24/7 across all 50 states via instant video, scheduled video, or async text.

notes: AI-powered healthcare platform with strong engineering and product focus
